Registration for courses with continuous assessment
retrieved.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. - Alternative place available (Alternativplatz verfügbar): You have not gained a place on the course of your interest, but will be able to attend another group (parallel course). You can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. - In process (In Bearbeitung): The registration period has expired and the allocation of places or your record is being processed. This can take several hours. In the meanwhile, you cannot make any changes. - Closed (geschlossen): At the end of the semester, all registrations are closed, which enables you to register for the same or a similar course or exam without any problems in the following semester. - **No place received** (Keinen Platz erhalten): You have been on the waiting list of a course/exam and did not receive a place. You can no longer move up the list. You can register for an equal course/exam in the following semester. retrieved.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
retrieved.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)

reranked.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
reranked.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.
